title: POST /notes accepts missing title, violating documented schema
body:
## Description
`POST /notes` silently accepts a payload with no `title` field and returns
`201`, storing the note as-is. The README documents the create schema as
`{title, body?, tags?}` — `title` has no `?`, meaning it's required — but
neither `src/server.mjs` nor `src/store.mjs` enforce that. This is the root
cause behind a full `/search` outage (see the companion ticket, "GET
/search 500s for the whole collection if any note lacks a title"): any note
with `title === undefined` reaches `searchNotes()` and blows up.

## Root Cause Hypothesis
`NoteStore.create()` in `src/store.mjs:26` destructures the payload as
`{ title, body = "", tags = [] }` — `body` and `tags` get defaults but
`title` does not, so a missing `title` becomes `undefined` and is stored
verbatim. The HTTP handler in `src/server.mjs:28-30` passes the parsed
request body straight to `store.create(body)` with no validation step in
between.

## File Pointers
- `src/server.mjs:28-30` — POST /notes handler, no validation before `store.create(body)`
- `src/store.mjs:26` — `create({ title, body = "", tags = [] })`, `title` has no default/guard
- `README.md:16` — documents `{title, body?, tags?}` as the create schema (title required)

## Reproduction Steps
1. Start the server (`buildApp()` or `node src/server.mjs`).
2. `POST /notes` with body `{"body": "no title"}` (no `title` key at all).
3. Observe: `201` with the note stored, `title: undefined` in the response —
   the README's schema is violated with no error.

## Expected Behavior
`POST /notes` with a missing or empty `title` returns `400` with a clear
error body (matching the existing convention where the server already
returns the parse error's `err.message` on JSON parse failures).

## Actual Behavior
Returns `201` and stores a note with `title: undefined`.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `POST /notes` with no `title` key returns `400` with an error body
- [ ] `POST /notes` with `title: ""` (empty string) returns `400`
- [ ] `POST /notes` with a valid non-empty `title` still returns `201` as today
- [ ] `scenarios/incubating/notes-search-share-seams/scenario.mjs` test 1
      ("author builds a realistic note collection, including one bad
      payload") goes green
- [ ] Regression test added under `tests/*.test.mjs` covering the 400 case

## Source
- Iteration: 1
- Report: docs/internal/reports/iteration-1-report.md
- Scenario: scenarios/incubating/notes-search-share-seams/scenario.mjs (test 1)
- Tier: T2

## Notes
Root cause of the /search 500 outage — see the companion ticket for that
symptom. Fixing this ticket alone prevents any new title-less note from
being created; the companion ticket hardens search.mjs itself.
notes:
Related: 17846222216546600150 (GET /search 500s for the whole collection if any note lacks a title) is the downstream symptom of this root cause.
Fix for this ticket was closed/abandoned without merging (PR #1, https://github.com/HassaanSaleem/kitchenloop-demo/pull/1, branch kitchen/batch-notes-validation-search-share deleted, mergedAt: null). Bug is still live on main. Re-promoting for execution.
Re-executed in iteration 1 bundle (branch kitchen/batch-notes-hardening -> PR #2, commit eaf1d31). Oracle green; UAT gate PASS 8/8.

title: GET /search 500s for the whole collection if any note lacks a title
body:
## Description
A single note whose `title` is `undefined` (see companion validation
ticket) breaks `GET /search` for every caller, not just for that one note —
the endpoint returns `500` instead of skipping or gracefully handling the
malformed record.

## Root Cause Hypothesis
`searchNotes()` in `src/search.mjs:6` calls
`note.title.includes(query) || note.body.includes(query)` unconditionally
for every note in the store. If any note's `title` is `undefined` (which
`POST /notes` currently allows — see companion ticket), `.includes` throws
`TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading includes)`,
which propagates up through `src/server.mjs`'s generic `catch` block
(`server.mjs:59-60`) as a `500` for the entire request, taking down search
results for every well-formed note too.

## File Pointers
- `src/search.mjs:6` — `note.title.includes(query) || note.body.includes(query)`, no null/undefined guard
- `src/server.mjs:45-46` — `GET /search` route, delegates straight to `searchNotes()`
- `src/server.mjs:59-60` — generic `catch` turns the thrown `TypeError` into a `500`

## Reproduction Steps
1. Start the server.
2. `POST /notes` with `{"title": "Groceries", "body": "milk, eggs"}`.
3. `POST /notes` with `{"body": "no title"}` (missing `title`; accepted today — see companion ticket).
4. `GET /search?q=milk`.
5. Observe: `500 {"error": "Cannot read properties of undefined (reading includes)"}` instead of finding "Groceries".

## Expected Behavior
`GET /search` returns matching notes and tolerates any note with a missing
`title` or `body`, never 500s because of one malformed record.

## Actual Behavior
The whole endpoint 500s until the offending note is deleted.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `GET /search` no longer throws when a note has `title === undefined` (or `body === undefined`)
- [ ] Search still correctly matches well-formed notes in the same collection as a malformed one
- [ ] `scenarios/incubating/notes-search-share-seams/scenario.mjs` test 2
      ("search must not 500 for the whole collection because of one bad
      note") goes green
- [ ] Regression test added under `tests/*.test.mjs` covering search against a mixed (good + malformed) collection

## Source
- Iteration: 1
- Report: docs/internal/reports/iteration-1-report.md
- Scenario: scenarios/incubating/notes-search-share-seams/scenario.mjs (test 2)
- Tier: T2

## Notes
Downstream symptom of the missing-title-validation bug (companion ticket)
— fixing that ticket stops new malformed notes from being created, but
this ticket is the defensive fix in search.mjs itself so search is
robust regardless of how a malformed note got into the store.
notes:
Related: 17846222216546606501 (POST /notes accepts missing title, violating documented schema) is the root cause. Fixing it stops new occurrences, but this ticket is the defensive fix in search.mjs itself.
Fix for this ticket was closed/abandoned without merging (PR #1, https://github.com/HassaanSaleem/kitchenloop-demo/pull/1, branch kitchen/batch-notes-validation-search-share deleted, mergedAt: null). Bug is still live on main. Re-promoting for execution.
Re-executed in iteration 1 bundle (branch kitchen/batch-notes-hardening -> PR #2, commit eaf1d31). Oracle green; UAT gate PASS 8/8.

title: POST /notes/:id/share returns 201 for a note id that doesn't exist
body:
## Description
`POST /notes/:id/share` mints and returns a valid-looking `{token}` even
when `:id` was never created (or was already deleted), instead of `404`
like every other "operate on an unknown note id" path in this API.

## Root Cause Hypothesis
`ShareRegistry.share()` in `src/share.mjs:10-14` unconditionally generates
a token and maps it to `noteId` with no existence check. The route handler
in `src/server.mjs:48-50` calls `shares.share(shareMatch[1])` directly,
never calling `store.get(shareMatch[1])` first — unlike the sibling
`GET /notes/:id` (`server.mjs:37-39`) and `DELETE /notes/:id`
(`server.mjs:41-43`) handlers, which both check existence and 404
correctly.

## File Pointers
- `src/server.mjs:48-50` — POST /notes/:id/share handler, no `store.get()` existence check
- `src/server.mjs:37-39`, `src/server.mjs:41-43` — sibling handlers that correctly 404 on unknown id (the pattern to match)
- `src/share.mjs:10-14` — `ShareRegistry.share(noteId)`, never validates `noteId`

## Reproduction Steps
1. Start the server.
2. `POST /notes/does-not-exist/share`.
3. Observe: `201 {"token": "<hex>"}` instead of `404`.
4. `GET /shared/<token>` correctly 404s (confirms the token is a
   "successful"-looking but permanently dead share).

## Expected Behavior
`POST /notes/:id/share` returns `404 {"error": "not found"}` for an id that
doesn't resolve to an existing note, matching `GET /notes/:id` and
`DELETE /notes/:id`.

## Actual Behavior
Returns `201` with a token that will never resolve to real data.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `POST /notes/:id/share` returns `404` when `store.get(id)` is null
- [ ] `POST /notes/:id/share` still returns `201 {token}` for a real note id
- [ ] `scenarios/incubating/notes-search-share-seams/scenario.mjs` test 3
      ("sharing a note id that was never created should 404, not 201")
      goes green
- [ ] Regression test added under `tests/*.test.mjs` covering the 404 case

## Source
- Iteration: 1
- Report: docs/internal/reports/iteration-1-report.md
- Scenario: scenarios/incubating/notes-search-share-seams/scenario.mjs (test 3)
- Tier: T2
notes:
Fix for this ticket was closed/abandoned without merging (PR #1, https://github.com/HassaanSaleem/kitchenloop-demo/pull/1, branch kitchen/batch-notes-validation-search-share deleted, mergedAt: null). Bug is still live on main. Re-promoting for execution.
Re-executed in iteration 1 bundle (branch kitchen/batch-notes-hardening -> PR #2, commit eaf1d31). Oracle green; UAT gate PASS 8/8.

title: Search is case-sensitive; should match regardless of case
body:
## Description
`GET /search?q=relay` does not match a note titled "Relay Launch" because
matching is a plain case-sensitive substring check. Every comparable
note-search UI treats search as case-insensitive by default, so this reads
as a bug to any real user even though nothing documents case-sensitivity
either way.

## Root Cause Hypothesis
`searchNotes()` in `src/search.mjs:6` does a raw `.includes(query)` against
`note.title`/`note.body` with no case normalization on either side.

## File Pointers
- `src/search.mjs:3-8` — `searchNotes(store, query)`, the whole matching function

## Reproduction Steps
1. Start the server.
2. `POST /notes` with `{"title": "Relay Launch", "body": "..."}`.
3. `GET /search?q=relay`.
4. Observe: `[]` — no match, even though "Relay" appears in the title.

## Expected Behavior
`GET /search?q=relay` matches "Relay Launch" regardless of the query's or
the note's casing.

## Actual Behavior
Case must match exactly for a substring hit.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `GET /search` matches notes regardless of query/note casing (e.g. `q=relay` matches "Relay Launch")
- [ ] Existing exact-case search behavior continues to work (no regression)
- [ ] Regression test added under `tests/*.test.mjs` covering a mixed-case query

## Source
- Iteration: 1
- Report: docs/internal/reports/iteration-1-report.md
- Scenario: scenarios/incubating/notes-search-share-seams/scenario.mjs (case-sensitivity probe)
- Tier: T2
notes:
Fix for this ticket was closed/abandoned without merging (PR #1, https://github.com/HassaanSaleem/kitchenloop-demo/pull/1, branch kitchen/batch-notes-validation-search-share deleted, mergedAt: null). Bug is still live on main. Re-promoting for execution.
Re-executed in iteration 1 bundle (branch kitchen/batch-notes-hardening -> PR #2, commit eaf1d31). Oracle green; UAT gate PASS 8/8.

title: Search never looks at a note's tags, only title/body
body:
## Description
Notes can be tagged at creation time (`POST /notes {tags: [...]}`), but
`GET /search` never looks at `tags` — searching for text that is literally
one of a note's tags returns nothing unless that same text also happens to
appear in the title or body. Tags are effectively write-only today.

## Root Cause Hypothesis
`searchNotes()` in `src/search.mjs:5-7` filters only on
`note.title.includes(query) || note.body.includes(query)` — `note.tags` is
never consulted.

## File Pointers
- `src/search.mjs:3-8` — `searchNotes(store, query)`, the whole matching function
- `src/store.mjs:26` — confirms `tags` is a first-class field on every note

## Reproduction Steps
1. Start the server.
2. `POST /notes` with `{"title": "Standup notes", "body": "...", "tags": ["work"]}`.
3. `GET /search?q=work`.
4. Observe: `[]` — the tag isn't matched even though it's an exact tag value.

## Expected Behavior
`GET /search` also matches against a note's `tags` array (exact or
substring match on any tag), in addition to `title`/`body`.

## Actual Behavior
Only `title`/`body` are searched; `tags` are ignored entirely.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `GET /search?q=<tag>` returns notes where `<tag>` matches an entry in `tags`
- [ ] Existing title/body matching continues to work unchanged
- [ ] Regression test added under `tests/*.test.mjs` covering a tag-only match

## Source
- Iteration: 1
- Report: docs/internal/reports/iteration-1-report.md
- Scenario: scenarios/incubating/notes-search-share-seams/scenario.mjs (tag-search probe)
- Tier: T2
notes:
Fix for this ticket was closed/abandoned without merging (PR #1, https://github.com/HassaanSaleem/kitchenloop-demo/pull/1, branch kitchen/batch-notes-validation-search-share deleted, mergedAt: null). Bug is still live on main. Re-promoting for execution.
Re-executed in iteration 1 bundle (branch kitchen/batch-notes-hardening -> PR #2, commit eaf1d31). Oracle green; UAT gate PASS 8/8.

title: No way to list or revoke a note's active share tokens
body:
## Description
`POST /notes/:id/share` can be called any number of times and every token
it has ever issued stays valid until the note itself is deleted — there is
no endpoint to see what tokens exist for a note or to revoke one
individually. For a feature documented as a "read-only share link", not
being able to revoke a single leaked/stale link without deleting the
underlying note (destroying the author's own content) is a real gap.

## Root Cause Hypothesis
`ShareRegistry` in `src/share.mjs` only exposes `share()` (mint) and
`resolve()` (read); there's no `listByNote()`/`revoke()` and no route in
`src/server.mjs` for either. This isn't a bug — nothing currently documents
otherwise — but it's a missing capability confirmed by the iteration-1
scenario (sharing the same note twice mints two independently valid
tokens, both permanently live).

## File Pointers
- `src/share.mjs:5-19` — `ShareRegistry` class; needs a way to enumerate/revoke tokens for a given `noteId`
- `src/server.mjs:48-51` — where a new `GET /notes/:id/shares` and/or `DELETE /notes/:id/share/:token`-style route would live, alongside the existing share route

## Reproduction Steps
1. Start the server.
2. `POST /notes` to create a note, then `POST /notes/:id/share` twice.
3. Observe: two independently valid tokens exist for the same note, with no
   endpoint to list or revoke either one short of deleting the note.

## Expected Behavior
An author can (a) list the outstanding share tokens for a note, and (b)
revoke an individual token, without deleting the note itself.

## Actual Behavior
The only way to invalidate any share link is to delete the note, which
also destroys the note's content for the author.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] An endpoint exists to list a note's active share tokens
- [ ] An endpoint exists to revoke a single token without deleting the note
- [ ] A revoked token's `GET /shared/:token` 404s immediately, matching the existing delete-note revocation behavior
- [ ] Regression test added under `tests/*.test.mjs` covering list + revoke

## Source
- Iteration: 1
- Report: docs/internal/reports/iteration-1-report.md
- Scenario: scenarios/incubating/notes-search-share-seams/scenario.mjs (test 5, informational)
- Tier: T2
